title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
This project analyses reported crime incidents in Chicago from 2022 to 2024. It combines exploratory spatial-temporal analysis with forecasting and machine learning to understand where crime clusters, how patterns change over time, and which models provide the strongest short-term prediction.
Read the full report

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
This project analyses reported crime incidents in Chicago from 2022 to 2024. It combines exploratory spatial-temporal analysis with forecasting and machine learning to understand where crime clusters, how patterns change over time, and which models provide the strongest short-term prediction.
Read the full report

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
Research Focus

Where are crime incidents spatially concentrated in Chicago?
Do hotspots persist across 2022, 2023 and 2024?
How do crime counts vary by month, week, day and hour?
Can machine learning improve prediction compared with classical time-series models?

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
Methods

Chicago public crime records from 2022 to 2024.
Spatial cleaning and coordinate validation.
Monthly and weekly time-series aggregation.
STL decomposition and stationarity testing.
Spatial aggregation to grid cells.
Global Moran’s I and Local Indicators of Spatial Association.
ARIMA, SARIMA, naive baseline and Random Forest forecasting.
Model evaluation using RMSE and MAE.

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
Selected Outputs



Crime counts aggregated to a 1 km grid





Day-hour crime heatmap





STL decomposition of weekly crime counts





Forecast model comparison

title: Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
text:
Key Findings
Crime shows strong spatial clustering, with persistent hotspots in central, western and southern parts of Chicago. Temporal patterns are also regular: crime increases during warmer months and concentrates around evening and weekend periods.
For forecasting, the Random Forest model outperformed the naive, ARIMA and SARIMA benchmarks, suggesting that engineered temporal features can capture short-term variation more effectively than the classical statistical models tested in this project.
Back to coursework

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
This project examines how betting shops are distributed across London neighbourhoods and whether gambling exposure is associated with wider forms of social and housing vulnerability. The analysis uses bookmakers per 10,000 residents as a neighbourhood-level exposure measure and compares this with indicators including unemployment, economic inactivity, overcrowding and private renting.
Read the full report

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
This project examines how betting shops are distributed across London neighbourhoods and whether gambling exposure is associated with wider forms of social and housing vulnerability. The analysis uses bookmakers per 10,000 residents as a neighbourhood-level exposure measure and compares this with indicators including unemployment, economic inactivity, overcrowding and private renting.
Read the full report

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
Research Focus

How unevenly are bookmakers distributed across London neighbourhoods?
Do areas with higher economic or housing pressure experience greater gambling exposure?
Which neighbourhood factors most strongly predict bookmakers per 10,000 residents?
Where do gambling exposure and social vulnerability combine into cumulative spatial risk?

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
Methods

OpenStreetMap bookmaker and gambling venue extraction.
2021 MSOA boundaries for Greater London.
2021 UK Census neighbourhood indicators.
Point-in-polygon spatial joining.
Population-normalised exposure metrics.
Regression modelling and spatial visualisation.
Neighbourhood risk mapping for cumulative exposure and vulnerability.

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
Selected Outputs



Bookmaker density across London neighbourhoods





Gambling exposure levels





Regression effects for neighbourhood predictors

title: The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
text:
Key Findings
Gambling exposure is highly uneven across London. Many neighbourhoods contain no betting shops, while a smaller number of central and East London areas experience high levels of exposure. The strongest associations appear around private renting and economic inactivity, suggesting that gambling exposure is linked to wider housing and labour-market pressure.
The project frames gambling as a spatial inequality issue, not only an individual behaviour question. It shows how local planning, licensing and zoning decisions can shape everyday exposure to gambling environments.
Back to coursework

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
This project investigates spatial inequality in road casualty risk across London neighbourhoods. It combines Department for Transport casualty records, MSOA boundaries, population data and deprivation indicators to examine where annual casualty rates are highest, whether high-risk areas cluster spatially, and how well neighbourhood conditions can explain or predict casualty risk.
Download the notebook

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
This project investigates spatial inequality in road casualty risk across London neighbourhoods. It combines Department for Transport casualty records, MSOA boundaries, population data and deprivation indicators to examine where annual casualty rates are highest, whether high-risk areas cluster spatially, and how well neighbourhood conditions can explain or predict casualty risk.
Download the notebook

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
Research Focus

How unevenly are annual road casualty rates distributed across London MSOAs?
Do high-rate and low-rate MSOAs show significant spatial clustering?
Which spatial, traffic-context and deprivation variables help explain casualty risk?
Can tree-based models improve prediction beyond an interpretable OLS baseline?

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
Methods

Spatial joining of road casualty records to 2021 MSOA boundaries.
Construction of annual casualty rates per 1,000 residents.
Collision density and population density measures.
Global Moran’s I and Local Indicators of Spatial Association.
Nested OLS modelling for interpretable explanation.
Decision Tree and Random Forest regression.
Permutation importance and partial dependence plots for model interpretation.

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
Workflow



Methodology flow chart

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
Selected Outputs



Average annual road casualty rate per 1,000 residents across London MSOAs





Distribution of annual average casualty rates





LISA clusters of casualty risk





Random Forest observed vs predicted casualty rates





Random Forest feature importance

title: Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
text:
Key Findings
Road casualty risk is unevenly distributed across London MSOAs and shows clear spatial structure rather than random variation. The analysis identifies hotspot and coldspot patterns, confirming that local risk is shaped by neighbourhood context.
The modelling results suggest that spatial-intensity variables, especially collision density, are important predictors of casualty rates. Random Forest modelling improves predictive performance and captures non-linear relationships, although the highest-risk outliers remain harder to predict.
Back to coursework

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Profile
I am an urban planning and spatial data student interested in city regeneration, transport planning, urban spatial analysis and evidence-led design. My work combines planning knowledge with digital and analytical tools, including CAD, SketchUp, Photoshop, Illustrator, ArcGIS, QGIS, SQL, R, Python, NetLogo, machine learning and data mining.
I have a strong understanding of UK planning systems, development processes and site assessment, with experience producing clear recommendations for practical and operational decisions. I am a diligent, adaptable and collaborative learner, with experience coordinating teamwork and contributing within multidisciplinary planning, design and transport environments.

title: Coursework
text: Welcome! This page showcases a collection of my academic coursework from my Master’s Degree in Urban Spatial Science and my Bachelor’s Degree in Urban Planning and Development. Both completed works and projects currently in progress are included below.

title: Coursework
section: MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London
text:
MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London

Spatial Analysis - CASA0005 Geographic Information Systems and Science

TfL Vision Zero
This is a spatial hotspot analysis examining where deaths and serious injuries cluster across London in 2024 using casualty data provided by TfL. The analysis applied point pattern analysis methods and spatial autocorrelation methods to identify statistically significant hotspot and coldspot areas and discuss policy implications for Vision Zero.
Full report coming soon.


New York Evictions
This is a spatial hotspot analysis examining where eviction activities cluster across New York in 2020 using evictions data provided by NYC Open Data. The analysis applied point pattern analysis methods and spatial autocorrelation methods to identify statistically significant hotspot and coldspot areas, discussing areas to be prioritiesed for policy interventions.
Full report coming soon.

This project examines how betting shops are distributed across London neighbourhoods and whether exposure is linked to deprivation, housing pressure and labour-market vulnerability. It combines spatial joining, neighbourhood-level exposure metrics, regression modelling and risk mapping.

CASA0006 Data Science for Spatial Systems

Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
This project investigates spatial inequality in road casualty risk across London neighbourhoods. It combines casualty records, MSOA boundaries, population data and deprivation indicators with spatial autocorrelation, LISA clustering, OLS and Random Forest modelling.
View the project page: London Road Casualty Risk



CEGE0042 Spatial-Temporal Data Analysis and Data Mining

Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
This project analyses Chicago crime incidents from 2022 to 2024 using exploratory spatial-temporal analysis, spatial autocorrelation, hotspot mapping and predictive modelling. It compares naive, ARIMA, SARIMA and Random Forest approaches for short-term crime forecasting.

I hold a BSc Urban Planning and Development degree from Cardiff University. I am interested in city regeneration, transport planning, spatial analysis and evidence-led urban design.
My work combines planning knowledge with digital and analytical tools, including GIS, spatial data science, statistical analysis and urban design software. I am committed to applying digital analytics to navigate complexity and support inclusive, practical and long-term decision-making in the built environment.

title: 课程作品
section: MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London
text:
MSc Urban Spatial Science, University College London

Spatial Analysis - CASA0005 Geographic Information Systems and Science

TfL Vision Zero
该项目使用 TfL 2024 年伤亡数据，分析伦敦死亡与重伤交通事故的空间聚集位置。研究采用点模式分析和空间自相关方法，识别显著热点与冷点，并讨论其对 Vision Zero 政策的启示。
完整报告整理中。


New York Evictions
该项目使用 NYC Open Data 的 2020 年驱逐数据，分析纽约驱逐活动的空间聚集格局，并讨论哪些地区应被优先纳入政策干预。
完整报告整理中。

完整报告整理中。



CASA0007 Quantitative Methods

The Geography of Everyday Gambling Exposure in London
该项目分析伦敦博彩店在社区尺度上的分布，并探讨博彩暴露是否与贫困、住房压力和劳动力市场脆弱性相关。项目结合空间连接、社区暴露指标、回归建模与风险制图。
查看项目页面：伦敦博彩暴露



CASA0006 Data Science for Spatial Systems

Spatial Variation in Road Casualty Risk Across London MSOAs
该项目研究伦敦不同社区道路伤亡风险的空间不平等。项目结合道路伤亡记录、MSOA 边界、人口与贫困指标，并应用空间自相关、LISA 聚类、OLS 与随机森林模型。
查看项目页面：伦敦道路伤亡风险



CEGE0042 Spatial-Temporal Data Analysis and Data Mining

Spatio-Temporal Analysis and Prediction of Urban Crime in Chicago
该项目分析 2022 至 2024 年芝加哥犯罪事件，结合探索性时空分析、空间自相关、热点制图与预测模型，并比较朴素基线、ARIMA、SARIMA 与随机森林的短期预测表现。

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text: English

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
项目概述
该项目研究伦敦社区尺度上的道路伤亡风险空间不平等。项目结合英国交通部道路伤亡记录、MSOA 边界、人口数据与贫困指标，分析哪些地区年均伤亡率更高，高风险地区是否存在空间聚集，以及社区环境变量能否解释或预测伤亡风险。
下载 Notebook

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
研究问题

伦敦 MSOA 年均道路伤亡率分布有多不均衡？
高风险与低风险 MSOA 是否呈现显著空间聚集？
哪些空间、交通环境与贫困变量能够解释伤亡风险？
树模型是否能在 OLS 基线之外提升预测表现？

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
方法

将道路伤亡记录空间连接至 2021 年 MSOA 边界。
构建每 1,000 名居民年均伤亡率。
计算碰撞密度与人口密度。
Global Moran’s I 与 LISA 空间聚类。
嵌套 OLS 模型用于可解释分析。
决策树与随机森林回归。
排列重要性与部分依赖图用于模型解释。

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
工作流程



方法流程图

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
代表输出



伦敦 MSOA 每 1,000 名居民年均道路伤亡率





年均道路伤亡率分布





道路伤亡风险 LISA 聚类





随机森林观测值与预测值





随机森林变量重要性

title: 伦敦 MSOA 道路伤亡风险的空间差异
text:
主要发现
伦敦道路伤亡风险在 MSOA 尺度上分布不均，并呈现清晰的空间结构。热点与冷点说明本地风险受到社区环境与空间强度因素影响。
模型结果显示，空间强度变量，尤其是碰撞密度，是预测伤亡率的重要因素。随机森林模型能够捕捉非线性关系并提升预测表现，但极端高风险地区仍更难准确预测，需要更细致的地方解释。
返回课程作品

title: 陆南希（Nancy / Nanxi Lu）
text:
你好，欢迎来到我的个人作品集。
我是一名关注 城市规划 与 空间数据 的学生，研究兴趣包括城市更新、交通规划、城市空间分析与循证设计。

 
关于我 课程作品 简历

title: 陆南希（Nancy / Nanxi Lu）

text:
研究兴趣

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text: English

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text:
项目概述
该项目研究伦敦不同社区博彩店的空间分布，并分析博彩暴露是否与社会经济和住房脆弱性相关。项目使用每 10,000 名居民对应的博彩店数量作为社区暴露指标，并与失业、经济不活跃、住房拥挤和私人租赁等指标进行比较。
查看完整报告

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text:
研究问题

伦敦博彩店在社区尺度上分布有多不均衡？
经济压力或住房压力较高的地区是否面临更高博彩暴露？
哪些社区变量最能预测每 10,000 名居民对应的博彩店数量？
博彩暴露与社会脆弱性如何形成累积空间风险？

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text:
方法

OpenStreetMap 博彩与 bookmaker 数据提取。
2021 年 Greater London MSOA 边界。
2021 年英国人口普查社区指标。
点面空间连接。
人口标准化暴露指标。
回归建模与空间可视化。
累积风险制图。

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text:
代表输出



伦敦社区博彩店密度





博彩暴露等级





社区预测变量的回归效应

title: 伦敦日常博彩暴露的空间分布
text:
主要发现
伦敦博彩暴露高度不均衡。许多社区没有博彩店，而少数中心区与东伦敦社区暴露较高。私人租赁与经济不活跃与博彩暴露之间的关联最明显，说明博彩环境与住房及劳动力市场压力相关。
该项目将博彩视为空间不平等问题，而不仅是个人行为问题，并指出地方规划、许可与分区政策会影响居民日常接触博彩环境的程度。
返回课程作品

title: 参考资料与项目来源
text:
主要数据来源

OpenStreetMap bookmaker 与博彩地点数据。
2021 年英国人口普查社区指标。
2021 年 Greater London MSOA 边界。
英国交通部道路伤亡记录。
伦敦 MSOA 人口与贫困指标。
芝加哥公共犯罪记录。

title: 参考资料与项目来源
text:
主要方法

空间连接与社区暴露指标。
回归建模与模型诊断。
空间自相关与 LISA 聚类。
时间序列分解、ARIMA 与 SARIMA。
随机森林预测与时间特征构建。
树模型回归、排列重要性与部分依赖图。

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text: English

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text:
项目概述
该项目分析 2022 至 2024 年芝加哥报告犯罪事件，结合探索性时空分析、预测模型与机器学习，理解犯罪热点、时间变化与短期预测表现。
查看完整报告

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text:
研究问题

芝加哥犯罪事件主要集中在哪些空间区域？
2022、2023 与 2024 年热点是否持续存在？
犯罪数量如何按月份、星期、日期与小时变化？
机器学习模型是否能优于传统时间序列模型？

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text:
方法

2022 至 2024 年芝加哥公共犯罪记录。
空间清洗与坐标验证。
月度与周度时间序列聚合。
STL 分解与平稳性检验。
网格尺度空间聚合。
Global Moran’s I 与 Local Indicators of Spatial Association。
ARIMA、SARIMA、朴素基线与随机森林预测。
使用 RMSE 与 MAE 评估模型表现。

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text:
代表输出



1 km 网格尺度犯罪数量





星期-小时犯罪热力图





周度犯罪数量 STL 分解





预测模型比较

title: 芝加哥城市犯罪的时空分析与预测
text:
主要发现
犯罪事件呈现明显空间聚集，热点持续出现在芝加哥中心区、西部与南部部分区域。时间上，犯罪在温暖月份、傍晚与周末更为集中。
在预测任务中，随机森林优于朴素基线、ARIMA 与 SARIMA，说明经过设计的时间特征能够更好捕捉短期波动。
返回课程作品
